title: STATIC BLOOM
subtitle: The Riverboat · 7:42 PM · Friday Dinner
epigraph_upright: Friday dinner. The first arrivals are already at the long table. The neighbor's been here since two. The upper deck is in mid-season. Nicola is making the meal she was always going to make tonight.
epigraph_reversed: The cousin doesn't come. The son leaves between courses. The river widow's plate stays untouched. The upper deck wilts. The boat is quieter than it's ever been on a Friday.
scene_description: Five guests on the books. The first arrivals are already at the table setting it for you. The neighbor is on the lounge bench. The upper deck has herbs ready and a tomato ready and a rose that opened this morning. Bloom is at 1 already because you spent the afternoon out there. Harvest is at 2 from the pantry pulls Nicola did before the boat woke up.
direction_hint: Feed the table. Land four connections, get one harder mood through their visit, leap from any threshold.
opening_log_lines: the first arrivals are at the long table. they've started setting.
the upper deck smells like tonight is going to be a tonight worth remembering.
the neighbor is on the lounge bench. they've been here since two.

stagnation_thresholds.label: somebody at the table is checking their watch.
the meal is starting to lean.

doubt_thresholds.label: nicola at the burner, full kitchen, full table.
she is not sure the soup needs another season.
she questions the menu she chose for tonight.

notes: Easy-mode Empress scenario — Friday dinner, five named guests + a cameo on turn 5. Bloom starts at 1; harvest at 2. Win requires 4 connections, 1 hard mood (the son is preoccupied; the widow is lonely), bloom >= 3 (so PLANT/TEND on the garden deck is part of the win path), leap from any threshold.
ambient_pool: the boat shifts in its mooring.
the kettle is on. nobody put it on.
the herbs on the upper deck are louder than they should be.
